generation.generated_answer: A Map in JavaScript may miss entries right after an insert returns the key due to case sensitivity in UUIDs. When using a PostgreSQL query with `ANY($1::uuid[])`, the returned keys are always canonical lowercase, even if the input array contains uppercase UUIDs [1]. Therefore, if you insert a key-value pair into a Map using an uppercase UUID and then try to retrieve it using the same uppercase UUID, the Map will miss the entry because it's internally storing the lowercase version. To avoid this issue, ensure both the insertion and retrieval use the same case for UUIDs.
